disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset of shakiness, sweating, palpitations and lightheadedness shortly after the evening medication round
trigger_question_examples: When did you start feeling like this?
What were you doing just before it came on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on soon after the nurses gave me my evening medications — I went all shaky and sweaty and my heart started pounding.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A facility medication administration error — a fast-acting insulin was given in place of the intended long-acting insulin, and a blood-pressure tablet was given twice
trigger_question_examples: You mentioned a mix-up with your pills — can you tell me what happened?
Do the staff know which medicines you were given tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The nurse said I was given my quick insulin instead of my long overnight one, and that my blood pressure tablet was given twice today by mistake.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feeling faint / near-syncope on trying to sit or stand, currently unable to stand
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel like you might pass out?
Are you able to stand up or is the dizziness stopping you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When I try to sit up I feel like I'm going to black out, so I'm staying propped on the bed — I can't stand at the moment.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cold sweat, tremor and palpitations — an adrenergic symptom cluster the patient recognises from previous low blood sugars
trigger_question_examples: Have you ever felt exactly like this before?
Does this feel like when your sugar has gone low in the past?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — it feels just like the couple of times my sugar dropped too low before: sweaty, shaky and my heart racing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, no weakness or slurred speech, no fever — the patient screens negative for these when asked
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain, or weakness or trouble speaking?
Do you feel hot or feverish at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain, my speech and arms feel normal, and I don't feel hot or feverish — just shaky, sweaty and dizzy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Fully alert and able to speak and swallow normally; nursing staff are on site and able to act on instructions
trigger_question_examples: Are you fully awake and able to swallow a drink safely?
Is there a nurse with you right now who can help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm wide awake and can talk and swallow fine, and yes, there's a nurse right here with me who can help.
